GAMM and GLMM toy build

Silpa Soni Nallacheruvu

2026-04-01

Load the simulated Data

catss <- read.csv("../Data/simulated_data.csv")

Check complete cases

print(paste("Number of complete cases:", sum(complete.cases(catss))))
## [1] "Number of complete cases: 4384"

Create the output

catss <- catss[complete.cases(catss), ]
catss <- catss %>%
  mutate(SCZ18_sum = rowSums(select(., starts_with("SCZ18")), na.rm = TRUE))

Split the data into training and testing dataset

Based on their twin_id

test_size <- 0.25

# Get unique IDs
unique_ids <- unique(catss$twin_id)

# Sample test IDs
n_test <- floor(length(unique_ids) * test_size)
test_ids <- sample(unique_ids, size = n_test)

# Split dataset
test_df <- catss[catss$twin_id %in% test_ids, ]
train_df <- catss[!catss$twin_id %in% test_ids, ]

# Print summary
cat(sprintf(
  "Train: %d rows (%d IDs), Test: %d rows (%d IDs)\n",
  nrow(train_df), length(unique(train_df$twin_id)),
  nrow(test_df), length(unique(test_df$twin_id))
))
## Train: 3287 rows (2487 IDs), Test: 1097 rows (829 IDs)

Prepare the data for the model formula by creating interaction terms and factor variables

# get a list of columns with their prefix
SUD15_vars <- grep("^SUD15_var_", names(catss), value = TRUE)
SCZ15_vars <- grep("^SCZ15_var_", names(catss), value = TRUE)
PRS_vars <- grep("^PRS_var_", names(catss), value = TRUE)
ADHD9_vars <- grep("^ADHD9_var_", names(catss), value = TRUE)
ASD9_vars <- grep("^ASD9_var_", names(catss), value = TRUE)
ACE_vars <- grep("^ACE_var_", names(catss), value = TRUE)
SES_vars <- grep("^SES_var_", names(catss), value = TRUE)
PCA_vars <- grep("^PCA_var_", names(catss), value = TRUE)
Sex_var <- grep("^Sex_var_", names(catss), value = TRUE)

factor_terms <- paste(
  c(
    paste0("factor(", SUD15_vars, ")")
  ),
  collapse = " + "
)


covariate_terms <- paste(
  c(SCZ15_vars, ADHD9_vars, ASD9_vars, ACE_vars, SES_vars, PCA_vars, PRS_vars, Sex_var),
  collapse = " + "
)

# create interaction terms between SUD15 variables and all other covariates
interaction_terms <- paste(
  c(
    paste0("factor(", SUD15_vars, "):(", SCZ15_vars, ")"),
    paste0("factor(", SUD15_vars, "):(", ADHD9_vars, ")"),
    paste0("factor(", SUD15_vars, "):(", ASD9_vars, ")"),
    paste0("factor(", SUD15_vars, "):(", ACE_vars, ")"),
    paste0("factor(", SUD15_vars, "):(", SES_vars, ")"),
    paste0("factor(", SUD15_vars, "):(", PRS_vars, ")"),
    paste0("factor(", SUD15_vars, "):(", Sex_var, ")")
  ),
  collapse = " + "
)

Fit the GAMM model

gamm_formula_str <- paste(
  "SCZ18_sum ~",
  factor_terms, "+",
  covariate_terms, "+",
  interaction_terms, "+",
  "s(twin_id, bs='re')"
)

gamm_formula <- as.formula(gamm_formula_str)

gamm_model <- gam(
  gamm_formula,
  data = train_df,
  family = gaussian(),
  method = "REML"
)

summary(gamm_model)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## SCZ18_sum ~ factor(SUD15_var_1) + factor(SUD15_var_2) + factor(SUD15_var_3) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_4) + factor(SUD15_var_5) + SCZ15_var_1 + 
##     SCZ15_var_2 + SCZ15_var_3 + SCZ15_var_4 + SCZ15_var_5 + SCZ15_var_6 + 
##     SCZ15_var_7 + SCZ15_var_8 + SCZ15_var_9 + SCZ15_var_10 + 
##     SCZ15_var_11 + SCZ15_var_12 + SCZ15_var_13 + SCZ15_var_14 + 
##     SCZ15_var_15 + SCZ15_var_16 + SCZ15_var_17 + SCZ15_var_18 + 
##     SCZ15_var_19 + SCZ15_var_20 + SCZ15_var_21 + SCZ15_var_22 + 
##     SCZ15_var_23 + SCZ15_var_24 + SCZ15_var_25 + ADHD9_var_1 + 
##     ADHD9_var_2 + ADHD9_var_3 + ADHD9_var_4 + ADHD9_var_5 + ADHD9_var_6 + 
##     ADHD9_var_7 + ADHD9_var_8 + ADHD9_var_9 + ADHD9_var_10 + 
##     ADHD9_var_11 + ADHD9_var_12 + ADHD9_var_13 + ADHD9_var_14 + 
##     ADHD9_var_15 + ADHD9_var_16 + ADHD9_var_17 + ADHD9_var_18 + 
##     ADHD9_var_19 + ADHD9_var_20 + ASD9_var_1 + ASD9_var_2 + ASD9_var_3 + 
##     ASD9_var_4 + ASD9_var_5 + ASD9_var_6 + ASD9_var_7 + ASD9_var_8 + 
##     ASD9_var_9 + ASD9_var_10 + ASD9_var_11 + ASD9_var_12 + ASD9_var_13 + 
##     ASD9_var_14 + ASD9_var_15 + ACE_var_1 + ACE_var_2 + ACE_var_3 + 
##     ACE_var_4 + ACE_var_5 + ACE_var_6 + ACE_var_7 + ACE_var_8 + 
##     ACE_var_9 + ACE_var_10 + ACE_var_11 + ACE_var_12 + ACE_var_13 + 
##     ACE_var_14 + ACE_var_15 + ACE_var_16 + ACE_var_17 + ACE_var_18 + 
##     ACE_var_19 + ACE_var_20 + ACE_var_21 + ACE_var_22 + ACE_var_23 + 
##     ACE_var_24 + ACE_var_25 + ACE_var_26 + ACE_var_27 + ACE_var_28 + 
##     ACE_var_29 + ACE_var_30 + ACE_var_31 + ACE_var_32 + ACE_var_33 + 
##     ACE_var_34 + ACE_var_35 + SES_var_1 + SES_var_2 + SES_var_3 + 
##     SES_var_4 + PCA_var_1 + PCA_var_2 + PCA_var_3 + PCA_var_4 + 
##     PCA_var_5 + PCA_var_6 + PCA_var_7 + PCA_var_8 + PCA_var_9 + 
##     PCA_var_10 + PRS_var_1 + PRS_var_2 + PRS_var_3 + PRS_var_4 + 
##     PRS_var_5 + PRS_var_6 + PRS_var_7 + PRS_var_8 + PRS_var_9 + 
##     PRS_var_10 + Sex_var_1 + factor(SUD15_var_1):(SCZ15_var_1) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_2):(SCZ15_var_2) + factor(SUD15_var_3):(SCZ15_var_3) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_4):(SCZ15_var_4) + factor(SUD15_var_5):(SCZ15_var_5) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_1):(SCZ15_var_6) + factor(SUD15_var_2):(SCZ15_var_7) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_3):(SCZ15_var_8) + factor(SUD15_var_4):(SCZ15_var_9) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_5):(SCZ15_var_10) + factor(SUD15_var_1):(SCZ15_var_11) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_2):(SCZ15_var_12) + factor(SUD15_var_3):(SCZ15_var_13) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_4):(SCZ15_var_14) + factor(SUD15_var_5):(SCZ15_var_15) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_1):(SCZ15_var_16) + factor(SUD15_var_2):(SCZ15_var_17) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_3):(SCZ15_var_18) + factor(SUD15_var_4):(SCZ15_var_19) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_5):(SCZ15_var_20) + factor(SUD15_var_1):(SCZ15_var_21) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_2):(SCZ15_var_22) + factor(SUD15_var_3):(SCZ15_var_23) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_4):(SCZ15_var_24) + factor(SUD15_var_5):(SCZ15_var_25) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_1):(ADHD9_var_1) + factor(SUD15_var_2):(ADHD9_var_2) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_3):(ADHD9_var_3) + factor(SUD15_var_4):(ADHD9_var_4) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_5):(ADHD9_var_5) + factor(SUD15_var_1):(ADHD9_var_6) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_2):(ADHD9_var_7) + factor(SUD15_var_3):(ADHD9_var_8) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_4):(ADHD9_var_9) + factor(SUD15_var_5):(ADHD9_var_10) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_1):(ADHD9_var_11) + factor(SUD15_var_2):(ADHD9_var_12) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_3):(ADHD9_var_13) + factor(SUD15_var_4):(ADHD9_var_14) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_5):(ADHD9_var_15) + factor(SUD15_var_1):(ADHD9_var_16) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_2):(ADHD9_var_17) + factor(SUD15_var_3):(ADHD9_var_18) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_4):(ADHD9_var_19) + factor(SUD15_var_5):(ADHD9_var_20) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_1):(ASD9_var_1) + factor(SUD15_var_2):(ASD9_var_2) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_3):(ASD9_var_3) + factor(SUD15_var_4):(ASD9_var_4) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_5):(ASD9_var_5) + factor(SUD15_var_1):(ASD9_var_6) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_2):(ASD9_var_7) + factor(SUD15_var_3):(ASD9_var_8) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_4):(ASD9_var_9) + factor(SUD15_var_5):(ASD9_var_10) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_1):(ASD9_var_11) + factor(SUD15_var_2):(ASD9_var_12) + 
##     factor(SUD15_var_3):(ASD9_var_13) + factor(SUD15_var_4):(ASD9_var_14) +

## ACE_var_26                        -0.019250   0.176399  -0.109  0.91311    
## ACE_var_27                         0.009169   0.163511   0.056  0.95528    
## ACE_var_28                         0.181905   0.125925   1.445  0.14869    
## ACE_var_29                        -0.052808   0.161461  -0.327  0.74365    
## ACE_var_30                         0.112951   0.163857   0.689  0.49067    
## ACE_var_31                        -0.110763   0.162701  -0.681  0.49607

## factor(SUD15_var_5)1:Sex_var_1     0.257799   0.317428   0.812  0.41678    
## factor(SUD15_var_5)2:Sex_var_1     0.095516   0.505809   0.189  0.85023    
## factor(SUD15_var_5)3:Sex_var_1     0.470807   0.242608   1.941  0.05241 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                 edf Ref.df     F p-value
## s(twin_id) 0.001428      1 0.001   0.551
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  -0.0044   Deviance explained = 14.2%
## -REML =  10108  Scale est. = 27.929    n = 3287

Check the twin random effect

plot(gamm_model, select = 1, main = "Random Effect of Twin ID in GAMM Model")

Evaluate the model predictions

gamm_y_pred <- predict(gamm_model, newdata = test_df, type = "response")
y_true <- test_df$SCZ18_sum


gamm_rmse <- sqrt(mean((y_true - gamm_y_pred)^2))
gamm_mae <- mean(abs(y_true - gamm_y_pred))
gamm_r2 <- 1 - sum((y_true - gamm_y_pred)^2) / sum((y_true - mean(y_true))^2)
gamm_corr <- cor(y_true, gamm_y_pred)

cat("GAMM RMSE:", gamm_rmse, "\n")
## GAMM RMSE: 5.634018
cat("GAMM MAE:", gamm_mae, "\n")
## GAMM MAE: 4.505792
cat("GAMM R2:", gamm_r2, "\n")
## GAMM R2: -0.1544634
cat("GAMM Correlation:", gamm_corr, "\n")
## GAMM Correlation: 0.01983276

Plot predicted vs true values

plot(y_true, gamm_y_pred, main = "Predicted vs True Values in GAMM Model", xlab = "True Values", ylab = "Predicted Values")
abline(a = 0, b = 1, col = "red") # indicating perfect predictions

Fit a GLMM model

glmm_formula_str <- paste(
  "SCZ18_sum ~",
  factor_terms, "+",
  covariate_terms, "+",
  interaction_terms, "+",
  "(1 | twin_id)"
)

glmm_formula <- as.formula(glmm_formula_str)

glmm_model <- glmer(
  glmm_formula,
  data = train_df
)
## Warning in glmer(glmm_formula, data = train_df): calling glmer() with
## family=gaussian (identity link) as a shortcut to lmer() is deprecated; please
## call lmer() directly
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
summary(glmm_model)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: 
## SCZ18_sum ~ factor(SUD15_var_1) + factor(SUD15_var_2) + factor(SUD15_var_3) +  
##     factor(SUD15_var_4) + factor(SUD15_var_5) + SCZ15_var_1 +  
##     SCZ15_var_2 + SCZ15_var_3 + SCZ15_var_4 + SCZ15_var_5 + SCZ15_var_6 +  
##     SCZ15_var_7 + SCZ15_var_8 + SCZ15_var_9 + SCZ15_var_10 +  
##     SCZ15_var_11 + SCZ15_var_12 + SCZ15_var_13 + SCZ15_var_14 +  
##     SCZ15_var_15 + SCZ15_var_16 + SCZ15_var_17 + SCZ15_var_18 +  
##     SCZ15_var_19 + SCZ15_var_20 + SCZ15_var_21 + SCZ15_var_22 +  
##     SCZ15_var_23 + SCZ15_var_24 + SCZ15_var_25 + ADHD9_var_1 +  
##     ADHD9_var_2 + ADHD9_var_3 + ADHD9_var_4 + ADHD9_var_5 + ADHD9_var_6 +  
##     ADHD9_var_7 + ADHD9_var_8 + ADHD9_var_9 + ADHD9_var_10 +

## factor(SUD15_var_4)2:Sex_var_1    -0.031569   0.233380  -0.135
## factor(SUD15_var_4)3:Sex_var_1    -1.072737   0.726542  -1.476
## factor(SUD15_var_5)1:Sex_var_1     0.257800   0.317428   0.812
## factor(SUD15_var_5)2:Sex_var_1     0.095514   0.505809   0.189
## factor(SUD15_var_5)3:Sex_var_1     0.470800   0.242608   1.941
## 
## Correlation matrix not shown by default, as p = 481 > 12.
## Use print(x, correlation=TRUE)  or
##     vcov(x)        if you need it
## optimizer (nloptwrap) convergence code: 0 (OK)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')

Check the twin random effect

plot(glmm_model, select = 1, main = "Random Effect of Twin ID in GLMM Model")

Evaluate the model predictions

glmm_y_pred <- predict(glmm_model, newdata = test_df, allow.new.levels = TRUE, type = "response")
y_true <- test_df$SCZ18_sum


glmm_rmse <- sqrt(mean((y_true - glmm_y_pred)^2))
glmm_mae <- mean(abs(y_true - glmm_y_pred))
glmm_r2 <- 1 - sum((y_true - glmm_y_pred)^2) / sum((y_true - mean(y_true))^2)
glmm_corr <- cor(y_true, glmm_y_pred)

cat("GLMM RMSE:", glmm_rmse, "\n")
## GLMM RMSE: 5.634023
cat("GLMM MAE:", glmm_mae, "\n")
## GLMM MAE: 4.505797
cat("GLMM R2:", glmm_r2, "\n")
## GLMM R2: -0.1544652
cat("GLMM Correlation:", glmm_corr, "\n")
## GLMM Correlation: 0.01983229

Plot predicted vs true values

plot(y_true, glmm_y_pred, main = "Predicted vs True Values in GLMM Model", xlab = "True Values", ylab = "Predicted Values")
abline(a = 0, b = 1, col = "red") # indicating perfect predictions
